description: Agent Structural Refactor - Compatibility cleanup and Linux/Windows platform normalization without intentional external contract changes

userStories.title: Define canonical executable entrypoints
userStories.description: As an agent platform engineer, I need one canonical entrypoint per executable so ownership is explicit and wrappers are not treated as core logic.
acceptanceCriteria: Identify canonical entrypoint files for agent and supervisor inside `apps/agent/src/**`
Each executable has exactly one real `main()` entrypoint in production code
Any remaining bootstrap files are classified as wrapper or canonical in code comments or file naming
No canonical logic is duplicated across multiple entrypoint files
`pnpm --filter agent type-check` passes

userStories.title: Remove redundant internal compat wrappers
userStories.description: As an agent maintainer, I need internal wrappers removed so only externally-required wrappers remain.
acceptanceCriteria: Redundant wrappers that only forward to another internal entrypoint are removed from `apps/agent/src/**`
Required external wrappers follow the strict pattern `wrapper -> main()`
Wrappers contain no config parsing, no OS branching, and no path-resolution logic
Imports/scripts/tests are updated to reference canonical entrypoints
`pnpm --filter agent test` passes for affected entrypoint tests

userStories.title: Create canonical agent path layout builder
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I need a single logical path layout owner so Linux and Windows do not duplicate layout definitions.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `apps/agent/src/platform/agent-path-layout.ts` with canonical logical directory and file names
Layout includes canonical names for releases/current/previous/logs/downloads/run and canonical state/config file names
The shared layout API is pure and testable (no direct OS side effects)
Unit tests validate expected layout shape for representative inputs
`pnpm --filter agent test` passes for new layout tests

userStories.title: Make Linux and Windows adapters consume canonical layout
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need OS adapters to consume shared layout so only true OS differences remain in adapters.
acceptanceCriteria: `linux.adapter.ts` and `windows.adapter.ts` consume the canonical layout builder
Logical path/file naming is no longer duplicated in both adapters
Adapters keep only OS-specific behavior (dataDir resolution, path joining, linking/pointer mechanics, extraction/control specifics)
Integration tests cover Linux and Windows layout resolution using the shared layout
`pnpm --filter agent type-check` passes

userStories.title: Split local control into OS/mode strategies
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I need local control split into focused strategies so behavior is testable and ownership is clear.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `linux-service-control.ts`, `linux-dev-process-control.ts`, and `windows-task-control.ts` under `apps/agent/src/platform/control/`
All strategies implement a shared control interface used by composition code
`local-control.adapter.ts` becomes thin composition/dispatch and no longer embeds full multi-mode control logic
Strategy unit tests cover start/stop/restart semantics for each strategy
`pnpm --filter agent test` passes for local control tests

userStories.title: Centralize config parse/validate/serialize APIs
userStories.description: As an agent developer, I need config logic centralized so runtime, CLI, and control paths cannot drift.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `apps/agent/src/config/agent-env.ts` for parse/serialize responsibilities
Create `apps/agent/src/config/agent-config.policy.ts` for validation, placeholder policy, and effective config resolution
Public APIs cover parsing, serializing, validating, and placeholder checks used by agent flows
Unit tests cover valid/invalid env input and placeholder-policy behavior
`pnpm --filter agent type-check` passes

userStories.title: Migrate consumers to canonical config owners
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need runtime/CLI/control-core to consume config APIs instead of duplicating parsing logic.
acceptanceCriteria: Runtime, CLI, and control-core use `config/*` APIs for parse/validate/serialize/resolve flows
Duplicate config parsing/serialization code in those consumers is removed
No new production imports depend on legacy internal compat paths
Regression tests verify effective config behavior remains stable
`pnpm --filter agent test` passes for affected config consumers

userStories.title: Enforce state ownership in state modules
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I need canonical state file persistence centralized in `state/*` so writes are consistent and auditable.
acceptanceCriteria: Canonical state file read/write paths are routed through `apps/agent/src/state/*` APIs
Direct writes to canonical state files outside `state/*` are removed
Non-canonical logs/artifacts remain outside `state/*` only when explicitly non-state
Tests verify canonical state persistence flows through state owners
`pnpm --filter agent type-check` passes

userStories.title: Add structural guardrails and confirm no contract regression
userStories.description: As the agent platform owner, I need guardrails and regression checks to keep boundaries stable after the refactor.
acceptanceCriteria: Add or update boundary checks/tests to prevent OS-branching spread outside `platform/*` and config/state ownership violations
Agent tests cover path layout, local control strategies, and config parsing boundaries impacted by this refactor
Smoke checks confirm wrappers still invoke correct `main()` entrypoints
No intentional HTTP/backend/UI contract changes are introduced by this refactor
`pnpm sanity` baseline and final results are recorded with no worsening versus baseline
